title: Psalms, Chapter 98
description: Psalms chapter 98. Both the manuscript page scan AND the Greek text are taken directly from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century AD) via the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). The Greek transcription preserves the actual uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra abbreviations, and lectional marks of the manuscript. Library folio: 114. English translation verbatim from the King James Version (1611, public domain) via bible-api.com.
introduction:
This entry meets the strict three-criteria standard of the Theosis Library, with the manuscript text drawn directly from the same source as the scan:

  1. Manuscript scan: The actual folio of Codex Sinaiticus containing this passage, from the Codex Sinaiticus Project (codexsinaiticus.org). Library folio reference: 114.
  2. Original text: The verbatim transcription of the Sinaiticus manuscript from the Codex Sinaiticus Project, preserving uncial forms, lunate sigma, nomina sacra (e.g. ⟨θϲ⟩ = θεός), and scribal marks. Nomina sacra are shown in angle brackets to indicate the manuscript's sacred-name abbreviations.
  3. English translation: The King James Version (1611), retrieved verbatim from the public-domain bible-api.com endpoint.

translation.original_text: <sup>1</sup>ψαλμοϲ τω δαδ κϲ εβαϲιλευϲεν οργιζεϲθωϲαν λαοι ο καθημενοϲ επι των χερουβιν ϲαλευθητω η γη <sup>2</sup>κϲ εν ϲειων μεγαϲ και ϋψηλοϲ εϲτιν επι πανταϲ τουϲ λαουϲ <sup>3</sup>εξομολογιϲθωϲαν τω ονοματι ϲου τω μεγαλω οτι φοβερον και αγιον εϲτιν <sup>4</sup>ϲυ ητοιμαϲαϲ ευθυτηταϲ κριϲιν και δικαιοϲυνην εν ιακωβ ϲυ εποιηϲαϲ <sup>5</sup>ϋψουτε κν τον θν ημων και προϲκυνειτε τω υποποδιω των ποδων αυτου αγιοϲ εϲτιν
translation.text: <sup>1</sup>O sing unto the LORD a new song; for he hath done marvellous things: his right hand, and his holy arm, hath gotten him the victory. <sup>2</sup>The LORD hath made known his salvation: his righteousness hath he openly shewed in the sight of the heathen. <sup>3</sup>He hath remembered his mercy and his truth toward the house of Israel: all the ends of the earth have seen the salvation of our God. <sup>4</sup>Make a joyful noise unto the LORD, all the earth: make a loud noise, and rejoice, and sing praise. <sup>5</sup>Sing unto the LORD with the harp; with the harp, and the voice of a psalm.

translation.original_text: <sup>6</sup>μωϋϲηϲ και ααρων εν τοιϲ ϊερευϲιν αυτου και ϲαμουηλʼ εν τοιϲ επικαλουμενοιϲ το ονομα αυτου επεκαλουντο τον κν και αυτοϲ ειϲηκουϲεν αυτων <sup>7</sup>εν ϲτυλω νεφεληϲ ελαλει προϲ αυτουϲ εφυλαϲϲον τα μαρτυρια αυτου και το προϲταγμα ο εδωκεν αυτοιϲ <sup>8</sup>κε ο θϲ ημων ϲυ επηκουϲαϲ αυτων ο θϲ ϲυ ευειλατοϲ εγενου αυτοιϲ και εκδικων επι παντα τα επιτηδευματα αυτων <sup>9</sup>ϋψουτε κν τον θν ημων και προϲκυνειται ειϲ οροϲ αγιον αυτου οτι αγιοϲ κϲ ο θϲ ημων
translation.text:
<sup>6</sup>With trumpets and sound of cornet make a joyful noise before the LORD, the King. <sup>7</sup>Let the sea roar, and the fulness thereof; the world, and they that dwell therein. <sup>8</sup>Let the floods clap
their hands: let the hills be joyful together <sup>9</sup>Before the LORD; for he cometh to judge the earth: with righteousness shall he judge the world, and the people with equity.
